items.name: Dice set
items.description: This item encompasses a wide range of game pieces, including dice and decks of cards (for games such as Three-Dragon Ante). A few common examples appear on the Tools table, but other kinds of gaming sets exist. If you are proficient with a gaming set, you can add your proficiency bonus to ability checks you make to play a game with that set. Each type of gaming set requires a separate proficiency.

items.name: Pouch
items.description: A cloth or leather pouch can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. A compartmentalized pouch for holding spell components is called a component pouch (described earlier in this section).

traits: I'm full of inspiring and cautionary tales from my military experience relevant to almost every combat situation.
I'm haunted by memories of war. I can't get the images of violence out of my mind.
I've lost too many friends, and I'm slow to make new ones.
I can stare down a hell hound without flinching.
I enjoy being strong and like breaking things.
I have a crude sense of humor.
I face problems head-on. A simple, direct solution is the best path to success.
ideals: Greater Good. Our lot is to lay down our lives in defense of others. (Good)
Responsibility. I do what must be done and follow orders, even if I don't like them. (Lawful)
Independence. When people follow orders blindly, they embrace a kind of tyranny. (Chaotic)
Might. In life as in war, the stronger force prevails. (Evil)
Live and Let Live. Ideals aren't worth killing over or going to war for. (Neutral)
Nation. My city, nation, or people are all that matter. (Any)
bonds: I would still lay down my life for the people I served with.
Someone saved my life on the battlefield. To this day, I will never leave a friend behind.
I'll never forget the crushing defeat my company suffered or the enemies who dealt it.
Those who fight beside me are those worth dying for.
I fight for those who cannot fight for themselves.
flaws: The monstrous enemy we faced in battle still leaves me quivering with fear.
I have little respect for anyone who is not a proven warrior.
I made a terrible mistake in battle that cost many lives, and I would do anything to keep that mistake secret.
My hatred of my enemies is blind and unreasoning.
I obey the law, even if the law causes misery.
I'd rather eat my armor than admit when I'm wrong.

activeQuests.title: The First Step
activeQuests.description: You awake in a strange clearing. The air is fresh, but the silence is heavy. You must get your bearings.

codexEntries.title: Giant Weasel, the Cunning Maw of the Wilds
codexEntries.content:
**Size & Appearance**
The Giant Weasel towers at 7 to 9 feet from snout to tail tip, its lithe frame still retaining the sleek, sinuous silhouette of its diminutive kin. Muscles ripple beneath a coat of coarse fur that shifts in hue with the seasons—ranging from ash‑gray in winter to a mottled russet in summer. Its skull is elongated, jaw line reinforced with a double row of razor‑sharp canines that can shear flesh as easily as a blade. The creature's forepaws end in retractable claws, each as long as a short sword, capable of rending bark and armor alike. A faint, musky pheromone drifts from its scent glands, warning rivals and attracting prey.

**Biology & Physiology**
Though its size rivals that of a small pony, the Giant Weasel retains the metabolic efficiency of the true weasel. It is a hypercarnivore, its digestive tract shortened to a mere 1.2 meters, allowing rapid processing of meat and swift expulsion of indigestible matter. Its heart beats at a rapid 120–140 beats per minute, granting bursts of speed up to 50 miles per hour in short sprints. The creature's ears, set high on its skull, rotate independently, granting acute directional hearing that can detect the faintest rustle of a mouse in underbrush. Its eyes possess a reflective tapetum, granting limited nocturnal vision and an eerie, amber glow in moonlight.

**Rumored Ecology**
Legends among forest peoples speak of the Giant Weasel as a solitary apex predator, patrolling the deep thickets of the Emerald Vale and the crag‑lined borders of the Whispering Fen. Some claim they nest in abandoned badger setts, expanding the tunnels to accommodate their massive bodies. Others whisper that a few colonies exist in the high alpine meadows, where they prey upon mountain goats and the occasional wandering griffon chick. Their territories are said to be marked by a series of gnawed tree trunks, each bearing a distinctive V‑shaped bite pattern—an unmistakable signature of the weasel's powerful jaws.

**Behavior & Tactics**
A Giant Weasel is a relentless hunter, employing ambush, pursuit, and opportunistic scavenging. Its primary tactic is a lightning‑fast dash from cover, closing the distance to its target in a heartbeat before delivering a series of slashing claw strikes. If cornered, it will unleash a feral roar that can startle even the hardiest of warriors, buying precious seconds to retreat or turn the tide with a desperate, ferocious bite. The creature is also known to cache surplus kills in shallow burrows, returning later to feast—a behavior reminiscent of the humble weasel's habit of storing prey.

**Myths & Folklore**
In the taverns of the border towns, the Giant Weasel is spoken of as a "Shadow of the Hunt," a spirit sent by the forest gods to punish those who overhunt or desecrate sacred groves. Some druidic circles claim that the weasel can sense the flow of ley‑lines, and that an encounter with one foretells a shift in the balance of nature. A particularly persistent legend tells of a "Silver‑Fanged Weasel" whose fur shimmers like moonlight and whose bite can cure a cursed wound—though no reliable witness has ever returned to confirm the tale.

**Adventure Hook**
A small hamlet on the edge of the Whispering Fen has been plagued by livestock disappearances. The villagers speak of a massive, dark silhouette slipping through the mist at night, leaving behind a trail of torn hides and a lingering scent of iron. The local magistrate offers a modest reward for any who can track, subdue, or otherwise deal with the beast.

**Survivor’s Tale**
> *"I thought I'd met the end when the ground trembled beneath those thunder‑fast paws. The thing lunged, teeth flashing, and for a heart‑beat I felt the world slip from my grasp. I rolled into a thicket, the claws raked my armor, and the beast snarled, eyes like twin coals. I drove my spear into its flank, felt the flesh give way, and it retreated, a wounded shadow slipping back into the night. I live, but the memory of that amber stare haunts my dreams. Trust not the quiet woods, for the Giant Weasel watches from the darkness, ever patient, ever hungry.*

codexEntries.title: The Unseen Maw of the Void
codexEntries.content:
**Physical Description**
The NonExistentMonster, colloquially known among wanderers as the "Void Maw," defies conventional anatomy. Witnesses describe a mass of shifting umbra, roughly the size of a horse, that seems to be composed of layered shadows rather than flesh. Its core glows with a faint, violet luminescence that pulses in time with the heartbeat of nearby living creatures. Tentacle‑like tendrils of darkness sprout from its periphery, each ending in a set of barbed, crystalline teeth that can slice through steel as easily as parchment. When the creature moves, the air around it grows cold, and the faint scent of ozone lingers, as if a storm were being born within its wake.

**Biology**
Scholars hypothesize that the Void Maw is not a true biological entity but a manifestation of raw planar energy, coalesced by the lingering echo of forgotten spells. Its "tissues" appear to be composed of condensed negative energy, sustained by the fear and despair of sentient beings. The violet core functions as a heart‑like nexus, drawing in ambient magical flux to repair damage and fuel its growth. The crystalline teeth are believed to be hardened shards of the Shadow Plane itself, capable of severing the soul‑threads that bind mortal life.

**Habitat & Ecology**
Rumors place the NonExistentMonster in the deepest chasms of the Blackened Vale, where the veil between worlds thins. It is said to haunt abandoned ruins, forgotten catacombs, and the crevices beneath ancient trees where the roots drink from ley‑line rivers. Though solitary by nature, a gathering of several Void Maws has been reported near a shattered moonstone obelisk, suggesting a possible breeding ground where the planar energies converge. The creature feeds on emotional resonance—particularly terror—absorbing the psychic echo of terrified victims to sustain itself.

**Legends & Myths**
Legends tell of a pact made long ago between a desperate sorcerer and the Void Maw, trading a village's souls for a single wish. The story ends with the sorcerer vanishing into a vortex of darkness, and the village never being found again. Some bards claim the Maw is a guardian of the forgotten, prowling the world to ensure that no secret remains unburied. Others warn that to speak its true name summons it from the shadows, and that the very act of naming it seals one's fate.

**Quote**
> "I thought the darkness was a trick of the night, until the Maw's tendrils brushed my cheek and I felt my own heartbeat echo back at me, louder than any drum. I ran, clutching the shattered rune that had kept it at bay, and I have never again set foot in the Vale. If you hear its whisper, turn away—let the void remain unfilled."
—Alara Windrider, survivor of the Blackened Vale expedition
